bugs.error_message: thread 'main' panicked at clap_complete-4.6.5/src/aot/shells/bash.rs:21:14: failed to write completion file: Os { code: 32, kind: BrokenPipe, message: 'Broken pipe' }
bugs.root_cause: clap_complete 4.6.5 bash backend calls .expect() on every write; under static-musl, piping the completion output to a reader that closes early (e.g. | head -5) triggers EPIPE, panic, exit 101. Glibc masks this because the ~25 KB output fits in the kernel pipe buffer before head closes; static-musl exposes the race. Initially attributed to fish backend only (bug not logged at that time, cerebrum [2026-05-25] said other shells exit 0 cleanly).
bugs.fix: Added pub(crate) struct EpipeSwallowingWriter<W: io::Write> that wraps stdout, swallows io::ErrorKind::BrokenPipe on write/flush, sets pipe_closed flag, pretends successful writes for the rest of the session. Returns Ok(buf.len()) intentionally (Ok(0) would cause write_all to surface Err(BrokenPipe) which clap_complete .expect()s on, re-creating the panic). 4 TDD unit tests cover swallow + propagate-other-errors + flush + pass-through. Commit d9c5f1c + doc clarification in c61917b. After fix: both bash and fish | head -5 exit 0 cleanly on both glibc and musl.

bugs.error_message: CLI rules.d enumeration sorted lexicographically (files.sort()), disagreeing with fagenrules (ls -v) for 9- vs 10- prefixes
bugs.root_cause: bare Vec::sort() on PathBuf is lexicographic, not version-sort; '10-' < '9-' lexicographically but '9-' < '10-' in GNU ls -v natural sort. W04 correctness requires matching fagenrules load order exactly.
bugs.fix: added fagenrules_cmp natural-sort comparator in load_order.rs (digit-run-as-integer comparison + bytewise tiebreak for total order) + rewired resolve_targets to sort via fagenrules_cmp instead of bare sort()

bugs.error_message: cargo mutants -f src/<crate-relative> silently does not filter (tests whole crate)
bugs.root_cause: cargo-mutants 27 --file matches the entire workspace-relative path; a crate-relative glob (e.g. src/load_order.rs) matches nothing in the workspace-relative path space and falls back to enumerating all mutants in the crate. --list also ignores the filter entirely.
bugs.fix: use --file <full workspace path> (e.g. --file crates/rulesteward-fapolicyd/src/load_order.rs) or --re <regex> for per-file targeting; for refactor-parity checks run whole-crate mutation and compare missed-counts against baseline

bugs.error_message: Stray rustc artifacts rust_out (4.3MB binary) + librust_out.rlib committed to git in 830e26a
bugs.root_cause: A snippet/API check ran `rustc <file>` without -o; rustc's default output names are rust_out / librust_out.rlib, which landed in repo root and got swept into a git add during the review-fix commit. .gitignore did not cover these names.
bugs.fix: git rm --cached rust_out librust_out.rlib; deleted from working tree; added rust_out + librust_out.rlib to .gitignore. Squash merge keeps the blobs out of main.

bugs.error_message: fapd-F01 false fatal on indented comments: parser treated '   # comment' as a parse error
bugs.root_cause: parse_line used strip_prefix('#') with no leading-whitespace strip; a '#' after spaces or tabs fell through to chumsky which produced fapd-F01
bugs.fix: Added trim_start() before strip_prefix('#') in parse_line. Any line whose first non-whitespace char is '#' is now correctly treated as a comment.

bugs.error_message: fapd-W03 false positive on full-line column-0 comments containing a second '#' (e.g. '# see (#) here')
bugs.root_cause: inline_comment_index set seen_token=true on the leading '#', then treated the second '#' as an inline comment after a rule. Previously masked by the A1 parser bug (F01 aborted before W03 could run); became reachable after the A1 fix.
bugs.fix: Added a comment-line guard at the top of the W03 source scan loop: if the first non-whitespace character of the line is '#', skip it entirely (full-line comment - fagenrules strips these before fapolicyd sees them).

bugs.error_message: fapd-E05 false positive on type-mix sets (abc,1 or 1,abc) that are valid on fapolicyd 1.3.2 and 1.4.3
bugs.root_cause: E05 used a symmetric 'has both a numeric-looking and a non-numeric value' test. fapolicyd set-type validity is version-divergent: 1.3.2 types by first value; 1.4.5 types INT only if all values fit i64; 1.4.3 does essentially no validation. The planned looks_numeric fix would have been backwards (silencing a set 1.4.5 rejects).
bugs.fix: E05 now fires ONLY when an INT-typed set (first value all-digits) contains an all-digit member exceeding i64::MAX. Type-mix (abc,1 / 1,abc) is intentionally not flagged (version-divergent). The full usage/version-aware check is deferred to a future --target rhel8|9|10 umbrella.

bugs.error_message: fapd-W04 false negative: two 20+ digit filename prefixes saturate to u64::MAX, compare Equal, fall through to bytewise tiebreak in the WRONG order from fagenrules
bugs.root_cause: take_number parsed digit-run prefixes into u64 with saturating_mul/saturating_add. Two filenames with different 20+ digit prefixes both saturate to u64::MAX, tie, and the bytewise fallback orders them opposite to fagenrules '/bin/ls -1v' order.
bugs.fix: Replaced take_number (u64, saturating) with take_digit_run + cmp_digit_runs. cmp_digit_runs: strip leading zeros, compare by significant-digit count, then bytewise. Exact for any digit-run length, no overflow possible.

bugs.error_message: Diagnostic column always 1 even for mid-line spans; JSON and human output could disagree for future non-rule-anchored codes
bugs.root_cause: Diagnostic::new hardcoded column: 1 at all call sites. For rule-anchored codes (rules start at line-start) column 1 is correct, so zero snapshots changed - but the invariant was not enforced and future mid-line codes would silently produce wrong columns.
bugs.fix: Added fill_columns(diags, source) to span_util. Iterates diagnostics and backfills each d.column from 1-based line_col(&d.span, source). Called at end of lints::lint() and before Err return in parse_rules_file(). Diagnostics with 0..0 span (layout fatals) are skipped.

bugs.error_message: ariadne caret snippet missing / dropped on source files with multibyte UTF-8 (CJK comments) before the flagged token
bugs.root_cause: ariadne 0.6 indexes its Source by CHARACTER offset. RuleSteward passed BYTE offsets to Report::build and Label::new. For multibyte UTF-8, byte offset > char offset; ariadne's span lookup went out of range and silently omitted the box-drawing caret snippet.
bugs.fix: Added byte_span_to_char_span helper: source[..span.start].chars().count() and [..span.end].chars().count(). Both Report::build and Label::new now receive char-offset spans. ASCII sources are unaffected (byte == char for ASCII).

bugs.error_message: fapd-W08 false positive: fires on documented dir= keywords 'execdirs', 'systemdirs', 'untrusted'
bugs.root_cause: W08 checked all dir= values for a trailing slash. The three fapolicyd.rules(5)-documented keywords are not paths; they expand to built-in directory lists inside fapolicyd. Appending '/' would turn them into literal paths and silently break the rule semantics.
bugs.fix: Added DIR_KEYWORDS const ['execdirs', 'systemdirs', 'untrusted']. Added needs_slash() guard: exact keyword matches return false (no slash needed). Exemption applies to both AttrValue::Str and each expanded value in AttrValue::SetRef. Verified on 1.3.2/1.4.3/1.4.5: all three load without trailing slash.

bugs.error_message: fapd-C01 false positive on dotfiles (.gitkeep, .DS_Store, etc.) in rules.d directories
bugs.root_cause: enumerate_rules_files used fs::read_dir and included hidden dotfiles. fagenrules uses 'ls -1v | grep -v "^\\.")' to enumerate - explicitly excluding dotfiles. C01 was checking dotfiles for the NN- prefix convention and false-firing.
bugs.fix: Added dotfile guard in enumerate_rules_files: any file whose name starts with '.' is skipped before lint codes run. Mirrors fagenrules behavior.

bugs.error_message: fapd-E01 false negative: exe_dir and exe_type in SUBJECT_ONLY caused is_known() to return true; fapolicyd rejects both
bugs.root_cause: Both 'exe_dir' and 'exe_type' were in the SUBJECT_ONLY const, making is_known() return true. Runtime testing on 1.3.2/1.4.3/1.4.5: both rejected with 'Field type (exe_dir) is unknown'. Neither appears in fapolicyd.rules(5). Their presence was a false negative.
bugs.fix: Removed 'exe_dir' and 'exe_type' from SUBJECT_ONLY. Updated classify_known_subject_only test to use exe/uid. Added exe_dir_and_exe_type_are_not_known_attrs test. Added fapd-E01 trap fixture exe-dir-unknown.rules. Fixed fapd-F03 fixture one-modern-many-legacy.rules which had encoded the bug by using exe_dir.
